understanding.project_summary: Confirmed: AutoDocX is a FastAPI application that scans local Python projects, analyzes source structure, generates LLM-based structured project understandings, compares scans, and builds/stores standalone HTML documentation. The application exposes REST endpoints (under /api/projects/*) to trigger scans, comparisons, context building, understanding generation, and document generation/storage. It includes pluggable LLM providers (OpenAI and Anthropic) implemented behind a BaseLLMProvider interface and created via LLMProviderFactory. Storage components persist scans, understandings, and generated HTML documents to a workspace (paths referenced by constants such as DOCUMENTS_PATH and UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH). Confirmed entry point: app/main.py. Reasonable inferences: The system is intended for automating documentation generation for Python projects by combining static analysis (code parsing + project analysis) with LLM-generated natural-language structure and HTML rendering. The DocumentationSync endpoint implements a single-workflow convenience path (scan -> optionally compare -> generate understanding -> generate & save document) according to the endpoint docstring.
understanding.architecture_overview: Confirmed: The codebase is organized into an API layer (app.api.project_routes), configuration (app.core.config), data models (app.models.project using Pydantic), service modules for scanning/parsing/analyzing (app.services.code_parser, app.services.project_scanner, app.services.project_analyzer), context and understanding generation (app.services.project_context_builder, app.services.project_understanding_service), scan comparison (app.services.scan_comparator), document building and storage (app.services.document_builder, app.services.document_storage), and persistent storage of scans/understandings (app.services.scan_storage, app.services.understanding_storage). LLM integrations live under app.services.llm with provider implementations app.services.llm.openai_provider and app.services.llm.anthropic_provider and a factory app.services.llm.factory. The API uses FastAPI and returns Pydantic models defined in app.models.project. Confirmed that environment/config loading is performed in app.core.config (load_dotenv usage) and that constants like DOCUMENTS_PATH and UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH are referenced. Reasonable inferences: The system is synchronous (no async functions detected) and uses file-based JSON storage for scans and understandings (docstrings state workspace/locations). The LLM provider abstraction suggests the system constructs system/user prompts, sends them to a provider via generate_json, and expects JSON-parsable responses. Exact storage layout details beyond described file paths, concurrency model, and authentication/authorization are not present in the provided context.

execution_flow.title: Receive API request
execution_flow.description: A client calls one of the FastAPI endpoints (for example: POST /api/projects/documentation/sync or POST /api/projects/scan). Requests are validated against Pydantic models in app.models.project. The app entrypoint is app/main.py which mounts the router defined in app.api.project_routes.

execution_flow.title: Project scanning & parsing
execution_flow.description: When a scan is requested (explicitly or as part of DocumentationSync), ProjectScanner.scan_project lists files, computes file hashes, parses Python sources using PythonCodeParser.parse_file, and produces a stored_scan payload containing parsed file metadata and project-level statistics.

execution_flow.title: Persist scan result
execution_flow.description: The scan result is saved via ScanStorage.save_scan into the workspace storage path (the docstring indicates files are stored under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json). The API then can return scan metadata or contents to the caller.

execution_flow.title: Compare scans (optional)
execution_flow.description: If an earlier scan or document exists and an update flow runs (e.g., DocumentationSync or documents/update), the service retrieves previous scan(s) via ScanStorage.get_latest_scan/get_scan and runs ScanComparator.compare_scans to detect added/removed/modified files, symbols, routes and dependencies. Comparison results are used to decide whether documentation should be updated.

execution_flow.title: Build LLM-ready context and request understanding
execution_flow.description: When an understanding is required, ProjectContextBuilder.build_context converts a stored_scan into either detailed or LLM-optimized project context. ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding uses ProjectContextBuilder and build_project_understanding_prompt to construct prompts. The LLM provider is created via LLMProviderFactory.create_provider (choosing OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider) and generate_json is called. The provider's response is parsed (BaseLLMProvider.parse_json_response) and the resulting structured understanding is saved via UnderstandingStorage.save_understanding.

execution_flow.title: Generate HTML documentation and save
execution_flow.description: DocumentBuilder.build_html turns a stored_understanding into standalone HTML (escaping content as necessary). DocumentStorage.save_document saves the HTML file and metadata (DocumentStorage references DOCUMENTS_PATH). The API can return document metadata or serve the HTML file using FastAPI FileResponse.

module_responsibilities.responsibility: Confirmed: Exposes REST endpoints for scanning, comparing, generating context/understandings, generating/updating/listing documents, and the DocumentationSync workflow. It orchestrates service calls (ProjectScanner, ScanStorage, ProjectUnderstandingService, DocumentBuilder, DocumentStorage, ScanComparator, ProjectContextBuilder, UnderstandingStorage). Inferred: It contains helper utilities for error-status mapping and comparison-of-changes logic. Uncertainty: exact error-handling behavior, authentication, and request-to-service parameter mapping beyond the function signatures.

Confirmed: Loads environment variables (load_dotenv) and provides configuration helpers and directory initialization functions. Contains helpers to obtain LLM configuration: get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key, and get_llm_model. Inferred: Declares constants like DOCUMENTS_PATH and UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH referenced by storage modules; responsible for creating required runtime directories. Uncertainty: exact environment variable names and default values are not present in the supplied context.

Confirmed: FastAPI application entry point; mounts router from app.api.project_routes and exposes simple health endpoints (root, /health). Inferred: Starts the FastAPI app and sets router prefix '/api/projects' (api_routes are listed under full path /api/projects/*). Uncertainty: server startup scripts and how app is run (gunicorn/uvicorn) are not explicit in code context.

Confirmed: Defines Pydantic models (requests and responses) used across the API: ProjectScanRequest, ProjectScanResponse, DocumentGenerationRequest, DocumentGenerationResponse, DocumentationSyncRequest/Response, ProjectContextRequest/Response, ProjectUnderstandingRequest/Response, ScanComparisonRequest/Response, and summary/metadata types for scans, understandings, and documents. Inferred: These models are used for request validation and response serialization by FastAPI. Uncertainty: exact fields/types and validation rules are not included in the supplied summary.

Confirmed: PythonCodeParser.parse_file reads Python files and extracts structural information: imports, functions, async functions, classes, methods, FastAPI routes, content hashes, and syntax errors. Inferred: Provides per-file AST parsing and hash calculation to support change detection. Uncertainty: exact AST library usage and robustness to edge cases not present in the context.

Confirmed: ProjectScanner.scan_project orchestrates filesystem traversal, ignores configured folders, computes size and hashes, parses Python files using PythonCodeParser, and invokes ProjectAnalyzer to build project-level relationships. Inferred: Produces a single 'stored_scan' dict that other services consume. Uncertainty: ignore rules and how large projects are chunked are not detailed.

Confirmed: ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project builds project-level relationships from parsed files: module maps, internal dependencies, function/class/method lists, FastAPI router prefixes and resolved endpoint paths, and project statistics. Inferred: Aggregates per-file parser outputs into a cross-file representation used for context generation and comparison. Uncertainty: exact dependency graph format and algorithms used to detect dependencies are not shown.

Confirmed: ProjectContextBuilder.build_context converts stored_scan into structured context in either 'detailed' or 'llm' modes: 'detailed' retains full metadata and hashes; 'llm' produces smaller LLM-ready payloads. Inferred: Responsible for truncation/selection logic to keep LLM prompt sizes manageable. Uncertainty: exact heuristics for 'llm' mode are not present.

Confirmed: ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding builds LLM-ready context (ProjectContextBuilder), constructs prompts (build_project_understanding_prompt and PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT), creates LLM provider via LLMProviderFactory, calls provider.generate_json, parses and returns structured understanding. Inferred: It orchestrates retries and error handling around provider calls (not explicit). Uncertainty: rate-limiting, timeout and retry strategies for LLM calls are not visible in the provided context.

Confirmed: BaseLLMProvider defines the interface every LLM provider must implement: provider_name (property), generate_json (send prompts & parse JSON). Includes static helper parse_json_response for robust JSON extraction from model output. Inferred: Subclasses implement provider-specific API calls and response handling. Uncertainty: exact exceptions and error parsing strategies are not shown.

Confirmed: OpenAIProvider implements BaseLLMProvider using OpenAI Responses API and exposes provider_name and generate_json to request/model responses. Inferred: It wraps OpenAI.OpenAI client calls and maps responses into dict[str, Any]. Uncertainty: exact request parameters and model defaults are not present.

Confirmed: AnthropicProvider implements BaseLLMProvider for Anthropic Claude (Messages API) providing provider_name and generate_json. Inferred: Implements provider-specific request/response mapping. Uncertainty: exact API call patterns and model selection defaults are not shown.

Confirmed: LLMProviderFactory.create_provider selects and instantiates provider implementations (AnthropicProvider or OpenAIProvider) based on a normalized provider name. Provides normalize_provider_name helper. Inferred: Provides a central place to add new providers. Uncertainty: how unknown/unsupported provider names are handled is not included.

Confirmed: Provides prompt construction helpers such as build_project_understanding_prompt and likely constants like PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT (imported elsewhere). Inferred: Encapsulates prompt templates used when calling LLM providers. Uncertainty: exact prompt text and templating logic are not shown.

Confirmed: ScanComparator.compare_scans compares two stored scans and reports added/removed/modified files, symbols, routes and dependencies. Inferred: Produces a structured diff used by the API to decide whether to generate a new document or return existing one. Uncertainty: Granularity and output schema of the comparison are not fully described.

Confirmed: ScanStorage persists and retrieves scan JSON files under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json and supports list_scans, get_scan, get_latest_scan, and save_scan operations. Inferred: Uses file I/O for storage and returns dict metadata (including IDs). Uncertainty: concurrency, atomic writes, and error handling behavior are not visible.

Confirmed: UnderstandingStorage stores and retrieves LLM-generated project understandings under workspace/understandings/<project_name>/<understanding_id>.json and supports list, get, get_latest and save operations. Inferred: Persists the parsed JSON understanding and metadata. Uncertainty: retention policy, size limits, and validation are not shown.

Confirmed: DocumentBuilder.build_html converts a stored_understanding into an HTML string (uses html.escape where appropriate). Inferred: Produces a single-file HTML doc intended for serving or download. Uncertainty: templating details, CSS/assets bundling, and support for multi-page output are not evident.

Confirmed: DocumentStorage.save_document persists generated HTML and metadata (methods to list documents, get latest metadata, get metadata by id, and get document file path). It references DOCUMENTS_PATH from config. Inferred: Saves HTML files to disk and returns document metadata (IDs, timestamps). Uncertainty: file-naming conventions, cleanup of old documents, and serving performance are not detailed.

api_overview.purpose: Orchestrated workflow: scan project, find latest document, create initial documentation or compare and create new version when changes exist (response model DocumentationSyncResponse). Confirmed by function docstring.
Scan, analyze, and save a local project (response model ProjectScanResponse).
List all saved project scans (response model list[ScanSummaryResponse]).
Get the latest saved project scan (response model StoredScanResponse).
Get one saved project scan by scan_id (response model StoredScanResponse).
Compare two saved project scans (response model ScanComparisonResponse).
Build compact LLM-ready project context from a stored scan (response model ProjectContextResponse).
Generate and save structured project understanding using the configured LLM (response model ProjectUnderstandingResponse).
List saved project understandings (response model list[UnderstandingSummaryResponse]).
Get the latest saved project understanding (response model StoredUnderstandingResponse).
Get one saved project understanding by id (response model StoredUnderstandingResponse).
Generate and save HTML project documentation from a stored understanding (response model DocumentGenerationResponse).
Compare a new scan and create an updated documentation version (response model DocumentUpdateResponse).
List generated project documents (response model list[DocumentSummaryResponse]).
Get the latest generated project document metadata (response model DocumentSummaryResponse).
Serve or download generated HTML documentation file (returns FileResponse).
Health / root endpoint for basic check (registered on app in app/main.py).
Health check endpoint returning basic service status.

key_dependencies.purpose: Used to perform project scans via ProjectScanner.scan_project when scanning is requested (scan and sync workflows).
Persists and retrieves scan JSON payloads (ScanStorage.save_scan/get_scan/get_latest_scan) referenced by multiple endpoints.
Compares two stored scans to determine documentation-relevant changes (ScanComparator.compare_scans).
Converts stored understandings into HTML via DocumentBuilder.build_html prior to saving.
Saves generated HTML files and metadata; serves latest document information and file paths.
Parses individual Python files to extract functions, classes, routes and hashes (PythonCodeParser.parse_file).
Aggregates parsed file data into project-level structures and endpoint resolution (ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project).
Creates the concrete LLM provider (OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider) used to call the LLM.
Instantiates OpenAIProvider when OpenAI is the selected provider.
Instantiates AnthropicProvider when Anthropic is the selected provider.
References DOCUMENTS_PATH configuration constant to determine where to save generated HTML.
References UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH configuration constant to determine where to save LLM understandings.

risks_and_gaps.title: LLM credentials and provider configuration
risks_and_gaps.description: Configuration functions (get_llm_api_key, get_llm_provider, get_llm_model) are present, but the code context does not show secure handling of API keys, secrets rotation, or how keys are supplied. Runtime behavior if credentials are missing is not detailed.

risks_and_gaps.title: Synchronous blocking LLM and IO calls
risks_and_gaps.description: The codebase uses synchronous methods for scanning, storage, and LLM provider calls. Long-running scans or LLM requests may block worker threads/processes; no async or background job queue is visible.

risks_and_gaps.title: Concurrent access and atomic file writes
risks_and_gaps.description: Storage layers appear file-based (JSON + HTML) but the context does not show locking, atomic write, or consistency mechanisms for concurrent requests updating the same project, risking corruption or race conditions.

risks_and_gaps.title: Error handling and retries for LLM calls
risks_and_gaps.description: ProjectUnderstandingService delegates to LLM providers but the provided context does not reveal retry/timeouts/rate-limit handling. Network or provider failures and partial responses may not be robustly handled.

risks_and_gaps.title: Input validation and size limits for LLM prompts
risks_and_gaps.description: ProjectContextBuilder supports an 'llm' mode to reduce prompt size, but there is no confirmed information on token counting, truncation policies, or how very large projects are handled to avoid exceeding provider limits.

risks_and_gaps.title: Lack of authentication/authorization
risks_and_gaps.description: API endpoints are defined but there is no evidence of authentication, authorization, or access control in the provided modules. This could expose project data and LLM usage to unauthorized callers.

risks_and_gaps.title: Missing operational details (deployment, scaling)
risks_and_gaps.description: Startup scripts exist (start.bat, stop.bat) but the service's production deployment, concurrency model, and resource requirements are not documented in code context.

risks_and_gaps.title: Unspecified testing and validation coverage
risks_and_gaps.description: No tests were shown in the provided context. The robustness of parsers, analyzers, and comparison logic is uncertain without unit/integration tests.

recommended_document_sections.title: Overview and Quick Start
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain what AutoDocX does, how to run it locally (environment variables, start/stop scripts), and a minimal example workflow (scan -> understand -> generate document).

recommended_document_sections.title: Architecture and Data Flow
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Diagram and describe components (API, scanner, parser, analyzer, LLM providers, storage), module responsibilities, and the runtime flow (including DocumentationSync sequence).

recommended_document_sections.title: API Reference
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Auto-generated reference for all endpoints under /api/projects including request/response schemas (Pydantic models) and example payloads and responses.

recommended_document_sections.title: Data Models and Storage Layout
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document Pydantic models in app.models.project and file storage locations and formats (workspace/scans, workspace/understandings, DOCUMENTS_PATH), including sample JSON files.

recommended_document_sections.title: LLM Provider Integration Guide
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe how to configure LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic), environment variables required, adding new providers, prompt templates, and expected JSON output format and validation rules.

recommended_document_sections.title: Error Handling, Limits and Retry Strategy
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document known failure modes, recommended retry/timeouts for provider calls, prompt size/token limits, and how the application surfaces errors to API clients.

recommended_document_sections.title: Security and Secrets Management
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Prescribe how to manage API keys (example env variables), recommended secrets storage, least-privilege practices, and endpoint access control (authentication/authorization recommendations).

recommended_document_sections.title: Operational Guide
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Instructions for deployment (uvicorn/gunicorn), running in containers, resource sizing, scaling considerations for scanning and LLM calls, and log/monitoring recommendations.

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing Strategy and Examples
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Unit and integration test guidelines for parsers, analyzers, comparator, and LLM provider mocks, plus sample test cases and CI recommendations.

recommended_document_sections.title: Extending and Customizing
recommended_document_sections.purpose: How to add new LLM providers, modify prompt templates, change storage backends, or extend parsing/analyzing rules.
